accurate.why: All claims match the bill text or are accurate context (50 U.S.C. 1546a expedited procedures, IRGC, statute references all verified).

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; the 2026 events are correctly framed as the bill's findings, and figures match the text.

states_purpose.why: Opening sentence clearly states the resolution directs withdrawal of U.S. forces from unauthorized hostilities against Iran.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Correctly invokes the War Powers expedited procedures under 50 U.S.C. 1546a and the 1976 Act, though the resolution does not amend law but operates under it.

exceptions_conditions.why: Accurately captures the three rule-of-construction carve-outs (self-defense, intelligence sharing, assisting Israel/partners) and the authorization exception.

effective_dates.why: The bill specifies no effective date or sunset for the directive; the summary correctly notes no deadline is set.

major_provisions_covered.why: Captures the operative removal directive and the rule of construction exceptions faithfully.

objective_tone.why: Neutral descriptive language throughout, attributing characterizations to the findings without editorializing.

coherent.why: Well-organized into purpose, findings, operative section, and exceptions; internally consistent and clear.

concise.why: Compact and free of padding or repetition while covering the essentials.

correct_entities.why: Correctly names the IRGC, Strait of Hormuz, the statutes, and the parties (Israel/partners) consistent with the text.

correct_figures.why: Figures cited (over 50,000 personnel, seven combat deaths, hundreds wounded, February 28, 2026) all match the bill's findings.
